description: Modern Soldier Morphus table (Dark Designs (WB6), pp. 53-55). Military gear and tactics; weapons, helmets, and armor may be biomechanical. With any Modern Soldier feature: +1 to save vs Horror Factor and +1 to Perception Rolls to recognize ambushes, booby-traps, military weapons, and explosive devices.

entries.name: Body Armor
entries.description: This 'Bane is always wearing a modern body armor of some form. Regardless of the style, it is a full suit of combat armor complete with a helmet. While it looks like the genuine article, the armor is unable to be removed as it is part of the Morphus.
customOneOffs: +1 to Perception Rolls to recognize potential ambushes and booby-traps, as well as military weapons and explosive devices.
The armor provides no protection against damage from disease, gas attacks, possession, spells, Talents, magic weapons, or other supernatural powers (which ignore the armor's S.D.C. entirely).

entries.name: Camouflage
entries.description: This character's Morphus form is dressed from head to toe in camouflage clothing. This may be as simple as a set of modern 'digital camo' fatigues and grease paint to a full-fledged ghillie suit that incorporates local foliage.
customOneOffs: +1 to Perception Rolls to recognize potential ambushes and booby-traps, as well as military weapons and explosive devices.
Given 1D4 minutes and suitable materials, the Nightbane can prepare a personal hiding spot that is extremely difficult to detect (Perception Roll of 1D4+13 needed to spot the character).
-20% to opponents' ability to Detect Concealment, Detect Ambush, Tailing, or Tracking rolls when trying to locate this Nightbane (bonus does not apply if other people are with the 'Bane).
Gets automatic first initiative when ambushing an opponent from a hiding place (normal initiative penalty applies when not ambushing).

entries.name: Dress Uniform
entries.description: This Morphus is always decked-out in military finery which gives him an aura of authority. It may also make him look out of place. This uniform can be from any military organization that has ever existed or a fictional one.
customOneOffs: +1 to Perception Rolls to recognize potential ambushes and booby-traps, as well as military weapons and explosive devices.
The Nightbane gains one Modern Weapon Proficiency of choice, but it can only be used while in Morphus form.
+5% to Invoke Trust/Intimidate.
Disguise, Impersonation, and Undercover Ops are impossible unless impersonating a military officer in full regalia.

entries.name: Gas Mask
entries.description: The gas mask is part of the Nightbane's Morphus and probably permanently attached to his face. It can be any style of gas mask from WWI to modern day equivalents.
customOneOffs: +1 to Perception Rolls to recognize potential ambushes and booby-traps, as well as military weapons and explosive devices.
The character suffers a -1 penalty to general Perception Rolls (which cancels out the global +1 bonus except for the specific military/ambush situations).
While in Morphus form the Nightbane is immune to any airborne chemicals, gas attacks, fumes, stench or disease that is not magical in nature.
Even when the gas or disease is magical, the character is +4 to save against its effects.
Nuclear radiation is ineffective against this Nightbane and will not even irradiate personal equipment kept on the character's person.
Voice is a bit muffled, causing the penalty to Communications skills.

entries.name: Medic and First Aid
entries.description: This Morphus is a combat medic, which means a pair of surgical gloves and surgical mask covering the mouth and nose are likely to be part of it, along with a medium-sized kit of field medical supplies.
customOneOffs: +1 to Perception Rolls to recognize potential ambushes and booby-traps, as well as military weapons and explosive devices.
By spending one P.P.E., the Nightbane can conjure basic medical supplies from the kit (e.g., bandages, antiseptic, tongue depressors) by visualizing what is needed.
By spending 6 P.P.E., the Nightbane has a healing touch that stops bleeding, causes open wounds to begin to heal, starts any stopped heart, and instantly restores 4D6 Hit Points or S.D.C.
If the Nightbane Medic has any stigmata, it is likely to be bullet holes that never heal and regularly ooze blood, or bandages covering the head or face.

entries.name: Night Hunter/Nightvision Goggles
entries.description: This permanent fixture increases range to 3,000 feet (914 m) and enables him to see in magical darkness and shadows at half the usual range without need of a saving throw.

combatContextModifiers.note: -3 on Perception Rolls and -5% on all skill performance in well-lit areas and in daylight (dislikes bright lights/sunlight).

customOneOffs: +1 to Perception Rolls to recognize potential ambushes and booby-traps, as well as military weapons and explosive devices (stacks with the +2 Perception bonus from this trait).
Never afraid of the dark and impervious to even magical blindness.
Punches, kicks, and melee attacks with a handheld weapon do double damage to Shadow creatures, Nightlords, and all creatures from the Nightlands!

entries.name: Field Radio
entries.description: The Nightbane has a military-grade field radio and/or satellite phone on hand at all times while in Morphus form, or a cybernetic equivalent. This provides a 50 mile (80 km) radius of radio communications.
customOneOffs: +1 to Perception Rolls to recognize potential ambushes and booby-traps, as well as military weapons and explosive devices.
The radio communications can only be jammed by magical means like the Anti-Magic Cloud or Sanctum spell.
Double the range and halve the cost of using the Operator Talent if possessed by this Nightbane. Also double the range in the Nightlands.

entries.name: Personal Weaponry
entries.description: The Nightbane's Morphus may have a bit of a machine-like appearance and certainly wears a combat helmet and fatigues. These weapons always appear on the character when the Morphus form is assumed, fully loaded.

playerChoices.label: Primary Weapon
options: Light Assault Rifle: 4D6 damage, 1,969 ft (600 m) range, three 50-round clips.
Heavy Assault Rifle: 5D6 damage, 1,640 ft (500 m) range, three 30-round clips.
Light Machine-Gun: 6D6 damage, 3,280 ft (1,000 m) range, single 100-round belt.
Sniper Rifle: 5D6 damage, 5,280 ft (1,609 m) range, three 10-round clips.
Shotgun: 4D6 damage, 200 ft (61 m) range, 7-round capacity plus two reloads.
Submachine-Gun: 3D6 damage, 656 ft (200 m) range, three 30-round clips.

playerChoices.label: Secondary Weapon/Side Arm
options: Light Pistol: 3D6 damage, 200 ft (61 m) range, three 15-round clips.
Heavy Pistol: 4D6 damage, 160 ft (48.8 m) range, three 9-round clips.
Machine-Pistol: 2D6 damage, 160 ft (48.8 m) range, three 20-round clips.
Grenades (x3): 1D6x10 damage to a 15 ft (4.6 m) radius.
Survival Knife/Small Melee: 1D6 damage, may retract from wrist/forearm.
Large Melee Weapon: Machete, sword, etc. 2D6 damage, may retract from wrist/forearm.

customOneOffs: +1 to Perception Rolls to recognize potential ambushes and booby-traps, as well as military weapons and explosive devices.
The Nightbane has a bonus of +2 to strike and +1 to parry with any weapons from this feature.
All such weapons do an extra +2D6 damage against the Nightlords, their demonic minions, and all denizens of the Nightlands.
Expended rounds are replenished every 24 hours.
-5% on the performance of all skills except W.P.s, Military, Physical, and Espionage skills.

entries.name: Heavy Firepower
entries.description: The Nightbane's Morphus possesses heavy weaponry that by all rights shouldn't be one man-portable. This weapon might take the form of a multi-shot rocket pod, a heavy machine-gun, Gatling-gun, or even a miniaturized light artillery cannon.
customOneOffs: +1 to Perception Rolls to recognize potential ambushes and booby-traps, as well as military weapons and explosive devices.
Each attack with this beast counts as one attack. Ammunition necessary is provided at a rate of three shots per melee round.
Double the range and damage of the Darkblast Talent if channeled through this weapon.

entries.name: Biomechanical
entries.description: The Nightbane may have tank treads for legs, or a machine-gun or other weapon or piece of equipment for an arm.
customOneOffs: +1 to Perception Rolls to recognize potential ambushes and booby-traps, as well as military weapons and explosive devices.
Roll for or select a feature from one of the Biomechanical tables (including Aquatic and Gear-Head), then relate it back to modern military.
